parsed_evaluation.task_summary: Compare London and New York to determine which has higher GDP per square kilometer and which has a warmer average temperature in Fahrenheit.

rubrics.description: Use consistent and comparable definitions and data for GDP and area.
rubrics.criterion: The execution trace specifies the administrative units used (e.g., Greater London and New York City) and uses GDP figures for the same year and currency for both cities, with area values in square kilometers matching those units.

rubrics.description: Compute and compare GDP per square kilometer correctly.
rubrics.criterion: The execution trace shows GDP and area values and the calculation GDP/area to produce GDP per km² for both cities, includes the numeric results with units, and explicitly identifies which city has the higher GDP per km².

rubrics.description: Obtain and compare average temperatures in Fahrenheit.
rubrics.criterion: The execution trace provides the average annual temperature for each city in Fahrenheit (including a Celsius-to-Fahrenheit conversion if needed using F = C × 9/5 + 32) and explicitly identifies which city is warmer.

rubrics.description: Show all inputs and conversions used for reproducibility.
rubrics.criterion: The execution trace lists the numeric inputs used (GDP amounts, currency conversion if performed, area values, temperatures) and the conversion steps/results (e.g., currency rate applied, Celsius-to-Fahrenheit calculation), enabling the calculations to be reproduced.

task_objective: Compare London and New York: which has higher GDP per square kilometer, and which has a warmer average temperature in Fahrenheit?
